description: You are the newly hired Makerspace Coordinator for a nonprofit community organization. The organization has leased an apartment shell to convert into a neighborhood makerspace. Convert the pre-drafted shell into a fully furnished community makerspace. Your design must include at least three interior partition walls to separate activities and at least two doors for access between zones. Define and label at least four distinct zones (such as Woodshop, Electronics Lab, 3D Print Farm, Textile Studio, Common Workspace, or Tool Storage) and apply different floor colors or textures to at least three of them. Furnish the space with at least 40 items from the built-in catalog, including at least 10 workbenches/tables, 10 chairs/stools, 8 storage units, 6 lighting fixtures, and 3 decorative items. Save the completed layout to overwrite the starter file.
